is an American pianist specializing in modern classical music. She has premiered the works of dozens of composers on her Exploding Piano series.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Her recitals involve recitation, costume, theatrical elements such as lighting, and sets. Kathleen's intention is to augment and extend the piano recital, and to borrow from contemporary theater, film and dance to create a new context for modern classical music. She also performs works that extend the sonic world of the piano recital, by using electronics both live and pre-recorded, preparation of the piano, and playing inside the piano on the strings themselves.

Tommasini, Anthony (30 April 1999). ["MUSIC REVIEW; A Keyboard Revolutionary Detonates Another Recital"](https://www.nytimes.com/1999/04/30/movies/music-review-a-keyboard-revolutionary-detonates-another-recital.html). *The New York Times*. Retrieved 31 May 2014.

["Doctor Nerve with Kathleen Supové, Nick Didkovsky, and guests"](https://web.archive.org/web/20140531144244/http://jazztimes.com/guides/events/2863-doctor-nerve-with-kathleen-supov-nick-didkovsky-and-guests). *JazzTimes.com*. Archived from [the original](http://jazztimes.com/guides/events/2863-doctor-nerve-with-kathleen-supov-nick-didkovsky-and-guests) on 31 May 2014. Retrieved 31 May 2014.

["Kathleen Supové"](https://web.archive.org/web/20140531125627/http://pianospheres.org/?page_id=1100). *pianospheres.org*. Archived from [the original](http://pianospheres.org/?page_id=1100) on 31 May 2014. Retrieved 31 May 2014.

first_paragraph: **Kathleen Supové** is an American pianist specializing in modern classical music. She has premiered the works of dozens of composers on her Exploding Piano series.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Her recitals involve recitation, costume, theatrical elements such as lighting, and sets. Kathleen's intention is to augment and extend the piano recital, and to borrow from contemporary theater, film and dance to create a new context for modern classical music. She also performs works that extend the sonic world of the piano recital, by using electronics both live and pre-recorded, preparation of the piano, and playing inside the piano on the strings themselves.

Musical artist

**Kathleen Supové** is an American pianist specializing in modern classical music. She has premiered the works of dozens of composers on her Exploding Piano series.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Her recitals involve recitation, costume, theatrical elements such as lighting, and sets. Kathleen's intention is to augment and extend the piano recital, and to borrow from contemporary theater, film and dance to create a new context for modern classical music. She also performs works that extend the sonic world of the piano recital, by using electronics both live and pre-recorded, preparation of the piano, and playing inside the piano on the strings themselves.

As Anthony Tommasini said in *[the New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")*: "What Ms. Supové is really exploding is the piano recital as we have known it, a mission more radical and arguably more needed."[[2]](#cite_note-2)

A partial list of the composers commissioned or premiered by Kathleen Supové: [Louis Andriessen](/wiki/Louis_Andriessen "Louis Andriessen"), [Terry Riley](/wiki/Terry_Riley "Terry Riley"), [Joan La Barbara](/wiki/Joan_La_Barbara "Joan La Barbara"), [Randall Woolf](/wiki/Randall_Woolf "Randall Woolf"), Lainie Fefferman, [Carolyn Yarnell](/wiki/Carolyn_Yarnell "Carolyn Yarnell"), [Eve Beglarian](/wiki/Eve_Beglarian "Eve Beglarian"), [Neil Rolnick](/wiki/Neil_Rolnick "Neil Rolnick"), [Missy Mazzoli](/wiki/Missy_Mazzoli "Missy Mazzoli"), [Nick Didkovsky](/wiki/Nick_Didkovsky "Nick Didkovsky"), [Anna Clyne](/wiki/Anna_Clyne "Anna Clyne"), [Phil Kline](/wiki/Phil_Kline "Phil Kline"), [Lukas Ligeti](/wiki/Lukas_Ligeti "Lukas Ligeti"), [Kitty Brazelton](/wiki/Kitty_Brazelton "Kitty Brazelton"), [Aaron Jay Kernis](/wiki/Aaron_Jay_Kernis "Aaron Jay Kernis"), [Mary Ellen Childs](/wiki/Mary_Ellen_Childs "Mary Ellen Childs"), [Marti Epstein](/wiki/Marti_Epstein "Marti Epstein"), Dan Becker, Elaine Kaplinsky, [Dafna Naphtali](/wiki/Dafna_Naphtali "Dafna Naphtali"), Jed Distler, Nicholas Brooke, [Lois V Vierk](/wiki/Lois_V_Vierk "Lois V Vierk"), Marita Bolles, [Gene Pritsker](/wiki/Gene_Pritsker "Gene Pritsker"), [Robert Carl](/wiki/Robert_Carl "Robert Carl"), Rob Zuidam, Belinda Reynolds, Michael Gatonska, singer/performance artist [Corey Dargel](/wiki/Corey_Dargel "Corey Dargel"), composer/video v.j. Peter Kirn, and Gameboy composer Bubblyfish.[[3]](#cite_note-supove-3)

She has appeared with The Lincoln Center Festival, [The Philip Glass Ensemble](/wiki/Philip_Glass_Ensemble "Philip Glass Ensemble"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Bang On a Can Marathon](/wiki/Bang_on_a_Can "Bang on a Can"), Music at the Anthology, Composers' Collaborative, Inc., and at many other venues, ranging from concert halls such as Carnegie to theatrical spaces such as [The Kitchen](/wiki/The_Kitchen_(art_institution) "The Kitchen (art institution)") to clubs such as [The Knitting Factory](/wiki/The_Knitting_Factory "The Knitting Factory") and [The Cutting Room](/wiki/The_Cutting_Room "The Cutting Room").[[3]](#cite_note-supove-3)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Besides being a soloist, Kathleen is a member of the Nick Didkovsky's art-rock band Dr. Nerve.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) She also curates Music With A View, a free music + discussion series at The Flea Theater.[[3]](#cite_note-supove-3)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kathleen_Supov%C3%A9&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Education")]

M.M., [The Juilliard School](/wiki/The_Juilliard_School "The Juilliard School"); B.A., [Pomona College](/wiki/Pomona_College "Pomona College"). Piano studies with [Daniel Pollack](/wiki/Daniel_Pollack "Daniel Pollack"), [Rosina Lhévinne](/wiki/Rosina_Lh%C3%A9vinne "Rosina Lhévinne"), Josef Raieff, [Russell Sherman](/wiki/Russell_Sherman "Russell Sherman").
